rag.description: New-RandomNumber adds on-demand random value generation to automation workflows, enforcing range bounds and type safety. Flexible MinValue/MaxValue parameters and multiple numeric types enable dynamic data-driven tests with built-in validation and consistent behavior across executions.

qa.question: What is the core function of the New-RandomNumber plugin?
qa.answer: Generates random numbers within a specified range directly in automation workflows, supporting dynamic test data and decision logic without external scripts.

qa.question: What key features and parameters does the New-RandomNumber plugin support?
qa.answer: Supports MinValue and MaxValue bounds, NumberType selection (Byte, Integer, Long, Float, Double), and type defaults when bounds are omitted, enabling precise control over output ranges and formats.

qa.question: Where can I find the New-RandomNumber plugin’s code and documentation?
What are best practices for using the New-RandomNumber plugin?
qa.answer: Always define explicit MinValue and MaxValue to avoid unexpected defaults, choose the appropriate NumberType for precision needs, and validate ranges to prevent overflow or invalid inputs in workflows.

qa.question: How are rule definitions and parameter schemas structured in the New-RandomNumber manifest?
qa.answer: The manifest follows version 4 schema, defining a parameters array with detailed objects (name, type, mandatory, default, values), and includes examples showing how to call the macro in action rules.

qa.question: What does the manifestVersion field specify?
What is the plugin key for New-RandomNumber?
Who is the author of the New-RandomNumber plugin?
What aliases are defined for the New-RandomNumber plugin?
Which categories is the New-RandomNumber plugin listed under?
Which platforms does the New-RandomNumber plugin support?
What type of plugin is New-RandomNumber classified as?
What parameters are defined in the New-RandomNumber manifest?
qa.answer: Parameters include MinValue (Number, optional), MaxValue (Number, optional), and NumberType (String, optional, default “Integer” with values Byte, Double, Float, Integer, Long).

qa.question: What summary description does the manifest provide for New-RandomNumber?
qa.answer: On-demand random generation with configurable range and type, support for data-driven and boundary testing, and workflow streamlining by removing external scripts.

qa.question: What does the apiDocumentation field specify?
qa.answer: None — no separate API documentation is provided.

qa.question: What does the w3c field specify?
qa.answer: None — no W3C protocol compliance is declared.

qa.question: What content is highlighted in the plugin’s description section?
qa.answer: The description details purpose, features, RPA and testing use cases, and examples of generating and using random numbers in workflows.

qa.question: How does the New-RandomNumber plugin handle errors?
qa.answer: Validates MinValue ≤ MaxValue and checks NumberType values; invalid ranges or unsupported types produce descriptive runtime errors to prevent workflow failures.

description: ### Purpose
Generates random numbers within automation workflows on demand.
MinValue and MaxValue bound the output range; NumberType selects the numeric type — Byte, Integer, Long, Float, or Double.
Produces dynamic test data and drives conditional logic in RPA and automation testing.
Random number support is built directly into workflow rules, removing the need for external scripts.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature                  | Description                                                    |
| Random Number Generation | Generates random numbers within a user-defined range.          |
| Range Configuration      | Allows specifying MinValue and MaxValue to control limits.     |
| Number Type Selection    | Supports different types: Byte, Integer, Long, Float, Double.  |
| Workflow Integration     | Works directly in automation workflows without external tools. |
### Usages in RPA
| Use Case          | Description                                                       |
| Data Generation   | Populate RPA tasks with random numbers for data-driven processes. |
| Conditional Logic | Provide dynamic values for decision-making steps in workflows.    |
### Usages in Automation Testing
| Use Case            | Description                                                         |
| Data-Driven Testing | Use random values as inputs to test software with varied datasets.  |
| Boundary Testing    | Generate edge-case values to test system limits and error handling. |

edge_cases: CSS selector not matching any element
annotations.expected_result: The example generates a random integer at runtime using the NewRandomNumber macro plugin and inputs it into the element identified by ‘#inputField’ using the SendKeys plugin.
annotations.notes: Combines `New-RandomNumber` with `SendKeys`. `New-RandomNumber` generates a random integer without side effects; `SendKeys` consumes it and types it into the element.

description: ### Generate and Send Random Number
Invoke `New-RandomNumber` to generate a random integer at runtime and pass it to the `SendKeys` plugin.
Use the `SendKeys` plugin to input the generated integer into the element identified by the CSS selector `#inputField`.

annotations.expected_result: Generate a new random integer using the NewRandomNumber macro plugin and register it as a session-scoped parameter named ‘RandomNumber’ via the RegisterParameter plugin.
annotations.notes: Combines `New-RandomNumber` with `RegisterParameter`. `New-RandomNumber` generates a random integer without side effects; `RegisterParameter` consumes it and stores it as a session-scoped parameter.

description: ### Register Random Number Parameter with Session Scope
Invoke `New-RandomNumber` to generate a random integer at runtime and pass it to the `RegisterParameter` plugin.
Use the `RegisterParameter` plugin to register a parameter named `RandomNumber` with session scope using the generated value.

MinValue sets the smallest number that can be generated by the random number function.
Defining a lower bound ensures results stay within a known range.
Omitting MinValue causes the function to use the lowest value supported by the integer type.
Specifying MinValue helps prevent values that are too low for the intended use.
parameters.name: MinValue

description: MaxValue sets the highest number that the random number generator can produce.
Establishing an upper limit ensures outputs stay within a predictable range.
Omitting MaxValue causes the function to use the maximum supported integer value.
Defining MaxValue helps avoid values that exceed intended limits.
parameters.name: MaxValue

description: NumberType determines which numeric format the generator will produce.
Choosing the right type ensures output matches the needed precision and range.
Omitting NumberType results in Integer values by default.
Specifying NumberType prevents unexpected numeric formats in results.
parameters.name: NumberType

description: Byte generates whole number values between 0 and 255.
Choosing Byte reduces memory use for small-range data.
Using Byte fits scenarios like binary file operations.
values.name: Byte

description: Double produces floating-point values from 0.0 up to but not including 1.0 with high precision.
Using Double supports tasks requiring about 15 decimal digits of accuracy.
Choosing Double helps in scientific calculations and detailed simulations.
values.name: Double

description: Float produces floating-point values from 0.0 up to but not including 1.0 with moderate precision.
Using Float saves memory when about 7 decimal digits of accuracy are sufficient.
Choosing Float is useful for graphics and real-time applications.
values.name: Float

description: Integer generates whole number values without any decimal part.
Using Integer simplifies tasks that require counts or indexing.
values.name: Integer

description: Long produces whole numbers beyond the standard integer range.
Using Long prevents overflow in large-scale computations.
Choosing Long is ideal for timestamps and very large counters.
values.name: Long

summary: Generates random number values on demand within automation workflows.
MinValue, MaxValue, and NumberType parameters control the range and type — Integer, Long, Float, Double, or Byte.
Supports data-driven testing, boundary testing, and dynamic data population without external scripts.
Random value generation is resolved inline at runtime, removing the need for helper scripts in RPA and testing tasks.
